title: always_comb unique else if with --assert will cause linter error
body:
This is a similar issue to #3088

```systemverilog
module test (
	input logic in,
	output logic out
);
	always_comb begin
		unique if(in == 1) out = 1;
		`ifdef BUG
		else if (in == 0) out = 0;
		`endif
		else out = 1;
	end
endmodule

```
to test run:
```
$ verilator --lint-only --assert -DBUG test.v
%Warning-LATCH: test.v:5:2: Latch inferred for signal 'out' (not all control paths of combinational always assign a value)
                          : ... Suggest use of always_latch for intentional latches
    5 |  always_comb begin
      |  ^~~~~~~~~~~
                ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/LATCH?v=5.006
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off LATCH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 warning(s)
```
NOTE: this bug occurs only with --assert and an elseif 

Verilator 5.006 2023-01-22 rev (Debian 5.006-2)
also on 5.007
